rationale: The citing paper repeatedly uses Deng and Mela (2018) as core prior evidence in its binge-watching framing, especially for observational findings that continued viewing follows prior viewing and that ads can disrupt viewing continuity. It then positions its own contribution against that baseline by arguing prior work is observational and TV-series focused, while offering causal tests and generalization to stand-alone media. The cited paper therefore plays a meaningful role in conceptual setup and contribution positioning, not just a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Deng and Mela's observational TV-viewing findings to motivate a broader "rabbit hole" theory. It extends that line of work by providing causal experimental evidence and mechanisms (category accessibility, immersion, anticipated enjoyment) in stand-alone media contexts beyond serialized TV.
